questions.q: In which Indian city does the famous Iron Pillar stand?
questions.explanation: The Iron Pillar is located in Delhi, within the Qutb complex in the Mehrauli area.

questions.q: What is the Iron Pillar of Delhi most famous for?
questions.explanation: The pillar is renowned for resisting corrosion and remaining largely rust-free for more than 1,600 years.

questions.q: What is the Iron Pillar primarily made of?
questions.explanation: The pillar is made of wrought iron, which is why its resistance to rust is so remarkable.

questions.q: The Iron Pillar stands within the grounds of which famous Delhi monument complex?
questions.explanation: The Iron Pillar stands in the courtyard of the Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque within the Qutb complex.

questions.q: Roughly how old is the Iron Pillar of Delhi?
questions.explanation: The pillar dates to the 4th century CE, making it roughly 1,600 years old.

questions.q: During which classical Indian empire was the Iron Pillar created?
questions.explanation: The pillar dates to the Gupta period, often called the 'Golden Age' of ancient India.

questions.q: What language is the main inscription on the Iron Pillar written in?
questions.explanation: The principal inscription on the pillar is in Sanskrit, written in the Gupta-period Brahmi script.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the Iron Pillar above the ground?
questions.explanation: The pillar rises about 7.2 metres above ground, with additional length buried below.

questions.q: The Iron Pillar is sometimes nicknamed which of the following?
questions.explanation: Because it has resisted rust for over a millennium and a half, it is popularly called the 'Rustless Wonder'.

questions.q: The Iron Pillar was originally erected in honour of which Hindu deity?
questions.explanation: The inscription states the pillar was set up as a standard (dhvaja) of the god Vishnu.

questions.q: The Iron Pillar's inscription is most commonly associated with which Gupta king named in the text as 'Chandra'?
questions.explanation: The 'Chandra' of the inscription is most widely identified with the Gupta emperor Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya).

questions.q: On which hill or place was the pillar said to have originally been erected, according to the inscription?
questions.explanation: The inscription says the pillar was set up on the hill called Vishnupada in honour of Vishnu.

questions.q: What type of religious standard was the Iron Pillar intended to be?
questions.explanation: The pillar was a dhvaja-stambha, specifically a Garuda standard dedicated to Vishnu.

questions.q: Why does the Iron Pillar resist rust so well, according to modern metallurgical study?
options: It is coated in modern paint
It is made of stainless steel
A protective passive layer formed largely due to its high phosphorus content
It is kept indoors in a sealed case
questions.explanation: The iron's relatively high phosphorus content helped form a thin protective passive film (often called misawite) that slows corrosion.

questions.q: Which structure surrounds the Iron Pillar within the Qutb complex?
questions.explanation: The pillar stands in the courtyard of the Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque, India's earliest extant mosque.

questions.q: The Qutb complex, where the Iron Pillar stands, holds what international recognition?
options: It is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World
It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site
questions.explanation: The Qutb Minar and its monuments, including the Iron Pillar, form a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: Approximately how much does the Iron Pillar weigh?
questions.explanation: The pillar weighs roughly 6 tonnes (around 6,000 kg), a major feat for ancient iron production.

questions.q: What ornamental feature originally topped the Iron Pillar?
questions.explanation: As a Garuda standard for Vishnu, the pillar was originally crowned by an image of Garuda, now lost.

questions.q: How was the Iron Pillar most likely manufactured by ancient craftsmen?
options: Cast in a single mould of molten iron
Forge-welded from many smaller pieces of hot iron
Machined from a solid iron block
questions.explanation: It was made by forge-welding numerous lumps of hot wrought iron together, not by casting.

questions.q: Which medieval ruler is traditionally credited with moving the Iron Pillar and other materials to the Delhi/Mehrauli site?
options: Anangpal / early Delhi rulers and later Qutb-ud-din Aibak's builders
questions.explanation: The pillar was relocated to Delhi in the medieval period, and the mosque around it was built by the Delhi Sultanate's founders under Qutb-ud-din Aibak.

questions.q: What approximate purity of iron does the Iron Pillar contain?
questions.explanation: The pillar is roughly 98% wrought iron, with small amounts of phosphorus and other elements.

questions.q: Which feature near the top of the pillar suggests something was once attached above it?
options: A decorative bell-and-melon (amalaka-like) capital with a socket
questions.explanation: The pillar has a decorative bell-shaped capital with a socket where the Garuda image was once fixed.

questions.q: The thin protective corrosion-resistant layer on the Iron Pillar has been given which scientific name in metallurgical literature?
questions.explanation: Researchers named the thin protective film 'misawite' (an amorphous iron oxyhydroxide, delta-FeOOH) that forms over the phosphorus-rich iron and shields it from further corrosion.

questions.q: Which Indian metallurgist is especially noted for explaining the Iron Pillar's corrosion resistance through phosphorus-based passive film formation?
questions.explanation: Metallurgist R. Balasubramaniam of IIT Kanpur published influential studies attributing the rust resistance to a phosphorus-rich passive layer.

questions.q: Including the buried portion, the total length of the Iron Pillar is closest to which figure?
questions.explanation: The pillar's total height from top to the bottom of its base is about 7.21 metres, of which roughly 1.1 metres is buried below ground.

questions.q: What protective measure was introduced around the Iron Pillar to stop visitors from harming it?
questions.explanation: A fence was erected around the pillar to stop the once-popular tradition of encircling it with one's arms and to protect its surface.

questions.q: What is the approximate diameter of the Iron Pillar at its base?
questions.explanation: The pillar tapers from roughly 41 cm in diameter at the base to about 30 cm near the top.

questions.q: The 'Chandra' of the inscription is praised for victories in which region, helping scholars identify the king?
options: The Vanga (Bengal) country and lands beyond the Sindhu (Indus)
questions.explanation: The inscription credits 'Chandra' with conquests in Vanga (Bengal) and the region of the seven mouths of the Sindhu, matching Chandragupta II.

questions.q: Which dark, deformed area on the Iron Pillar is attributed to a historical cannonball or projectile strike?
options: A dent on the mid-shaft, with fissuring on the opposite side
A crack along the entire length
A missing top third of the column
questions.explanation: A dent about 4 metres up the shaft, with horizontal fissuring on the opposite side, is attributed to a close-range cannonball strike, traditionally linked to Nadir Shah's 1739 invasion.

questions.q: Why is the Iron Pillar considered important evidence about ancient Indian technology?
options: It proves use of gunpowder in the Gupta era
It shows early use of electricity
It contains the first written numerals
It demonstrates advanced ancient Indian iron-extraction and forge-welding skill
questions.explanation: Producing a 6-tonne, high-purity, corrosion-resistant iron column in the 4th century reflects remarkably advanced ancient Indian metallurgy.
